creator: michael.osipov@siemens.com
text:
(In reply to ports.maintainer from comment #2)

It does, that is not true. You can supply your own CA certs and block those from NSS, I am doing this. Even if there is a mismatch between a patch release and NSS you can apply the diff manually:
$ tree /usr/local/share/certs
/usr/local/share/certs
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ca-root-nss.crt

I have automated additional CA certs for our enterprise as soon as certctl(8) was available. A port does not contain tens fo certs and post-install rehashes them. It just works:
$ pkg info siemens-cacerts | grep Comment
Comment        : Collection of CA certificates trusted by Siemens


Ed,

this is for the Enterprise WG, releng needs to issue a patch release when there is a NSS DB update as well.
